title: HIV Testing Services Uptake in Adolescent Girls in Sub-Saharan Africa
summary: An E156 micro-paper and meta-analysis summarizing the pooled proportion of HIV testing uptake among AGYW in Sub-Saharan Africa.
primary_estimand: Proportion of adolescent girls and young women testing for HIV
data: Local raw_studies.csv in the data/ directory
source_article: Derived from literature review of 7 RCTs and cohorts
body: In adolescent girls and young women across sub-Saharan Africa, what is the pooled uptake of HIV testing services and which delivery models perform best? We conducted a random-effects meta-analysis of seven trial arms and cohorts involving 4,410 participants to estimate the pooled proportion of those completing a test. Seven studies met eligibility criteria covering HIV self-testing, sports-based community programs, integrated clinic services, and standard-of-care models across South Africa, Zambia, Malawi, and Uganda. The pooled proportion for HIV testing was 0.73, with a 95% confidence interval of 0.46 to 0.90 and high between-study heterogeneity. Choice-based self-testing and integrated health services achieved the highest testing proportions exceeding 0.92, whereas sports-based and standard clinic models showed lower overall coverage. These findings indicate moving testing out of traditional facilities and offering self-testing choices dramatically increases reach for this priority demographic. Interpretation is limited by the small number of randomized trials, varying definitions of recent uptake, and reliance on self-reported outcomes.

primary_plot.caption: Forest plot of Logit Transformed Proportions.
